G - Attention Check: How many seasons are there in a year?
Open feedback: If you experienced any accessibility or technical issues during this survey, or have additional feedback, please let us know.

E-High-Impression: The audio in the clip sounded more urgent than just taking a stroll to the grocery store. While the instructions were clear, they could have been more calm and relaxing.
E-Low-Impression: That was a much more pleasant experience. While not making me feel as if I'm in a high stakes situation, the robot was still clear in instructions and made me feel comfortable.
E-Mid-Impression: While the robot was polite and constantly said "Please", that made me feel a little bit uneasy as if the robot was asking for my permission to move to the location instead of directing me to the location. I was more worried about my own safety and whether or not I could trust a timid robot.
E-best(1H2L3M): While not being too assertive and not too timid, the robot in the second recording made me feel more comfortable on my way to the store. There was a sense of my robot and I are going on a stroll together.
E-worst(1H2L3M): Given that the situation was going to the grocery store with my robot, the first option was the lease acceptable just by how on edge their instructions made me feel. I felt as if I were being chased and for some reason we had to escape quickly.
E-difference: The tone between the two was vastly different, in one the tone was more calming and created a sense of unity where the other one was more assertive and stress inducing. With the second option, I felt more comfortable going on a stroll and in the first one, I felt as if I had to constantly look over my shoulder.
F-High-Impression: The robot knew what to do and asked for my input when necessary.
F-Low-Impression: The robot's voice reminded me of Google's GSP and also "cared" whether something or someone has passed me quickly once I told them, they recommended a different trail there but also said how much added time that would take. Letting me make a choice whether or not to follow through in which case I didn't.
F-Mid-Impression: While the robot may have been more pushy, it was also direct and gave me the option to bow out respectively of the option by not walking over there. Instead of giving choices that I would have to decided on my own this was a very dismissive approach.
F-best(1H2L3M): The approach in this option was direct, but not too assertive. Another thing I noticed too was the only one to put my mind at ease over what brushed past me.
F-worst(1H2L3M): The robot in this clip was more assertive but also more demissive. Making this one not as appropriate as the other
F-difference: A big difference was the difference in compassion and care between the two audio files. In the second audio clip the robot was more compassionate, putting me at ease over what brushed passed me and taking me into account. While the third option was more cold and directly approaching the issue as "If you want to go this way change direction" otherwise keep going.
G-Mid-Impression: The robot left me to make all of the decisions which I'm not quite sure why that matters if I'm unable to see where I'm going anyway. This caused a little bit of confusion for me. Although it sounded like we were going through a park together and the robot asked my opinion on every decision which I did appreciate I did not see the reasoning behind it.
G-Low-Impression: In this clip, the robot was much more sure of itself and only asked for my opinion based on what they perceived to be the correct answer and then left it up to me based on what it said would be the best route. The experience was still polite, but direct in approach.
G-High-Impression: The robot in this clip was cold towards me making the experience less enjoyable. While the directions were direct they were also brief leading me to make a decision without possibly knowing which is the more favorable outcome. C-High-Impression: My impression is that I would feel extremely relieved to receive these instructions. It states what the hazard is in a timely manner, and ensures my safety.
C-Mid-Impression: This robot is polite, and it feels like the situations are less dangerous based on how it is describing them? While it's kind of comforting to listen too, I'm not sure if it's sure.
C-Low-Impression: I have mixed feelings on this clip. It's kind of sweet that the robot frames it as "we'll do it together," but at the same time, I worry about the severity of the situation and response time. Also, the "we'll" was a bit repetitive.
C-best(1H2L3M): I chose this option not only because the instructions are objective and brief, but it could also lead to better response time in case there is something hazardous in the way. It speaks briefly enough that one could quickly respond, even before they fully process it.
C-worst(1H2L3M): I chose the second option because it spoke slowly and conversationally. While it still gives directions instead of suggestions, which I appreciate, they feel more lax, and too slow/wordy to have the listener react in time.
C-difference(1H2L3M): All in all, we have one that is objective and brief, one that is polite and repetitive, and one that felt very friendly (using the we'll). I appreciated how they all gave directions/stated facts instead of being wishy washy, but some were still shorter and more appropriate than others.
D-Low-Impression: It seemed very brief, and "straight to the point," but it was giving me a lot of information at once, which would confuse me if I were the one walking. I can't keep track of so many things.
D-Mid-Impression: It seemed extremely similar to the last one, but the voice sounded a bit more intimidating and straightforward. It felt very sure of itself, even moreso than the last one.
D-High-Impression: I love this one! It felt almost like a GPS app. I appreciate how objective it is, and it allows me to follow simple instructions without being confused or set off track by a conversation.
D-best(1H2L3M): I chose this option because it felt the most objective, straightforward, and easy to understand of the three. There is also a bit of familiarity involved, considering it sounds like a GPS app rather than a conversation. It doesn't hedge with "might" or "maybe" and there's no fluff. The instructions are brief, and this eases the mental load one might feel have while both walking and listening.
D-worst(1H2L3M): This version felt "wishy-washy," or confusing due to how much it hedged. Phrasing like "we may be" or "we might," or "we could," confuses me and may disorient someone as they are walking. It feels more like I'm being offered suggestions rather than actual navigation.
D-difference: The voice itself seems blunt, or assertive. But each one is a different amount of "wordy." One feels like a wishy-washy conversation. It feels polite but I'm straining to listen. The next feels cordial and brief, or even a little cold. And the last audio gave very simple instructions without fluff, which felt stabilizing in a way.
G-Mid-Impression: The robot continuously offered him other options, outside of the normal route. I feel like I would only want to hear about additional options if I asked. When it comes to the instructions/voice itself, I find it's adequate. It seems polite, willing, friendly. I like that they're presented as options.
G-Low-Impression: This has the same problem as the previous wordy instructions from the first three audio clips. It feels confusing/disorienting to listen to, and I imagine I would stop in the middle of sidewalks and ask for clarification.
G-High-Impression: This was very objective-sounding similarly to the one I rated highly from the first set. But somehow it feels slightly off here? I appreciate it, but I'm not sure it fits the best.
G-best(1H2L3M): Initially, I thought the third option was best because it was balanced, but listening in again, I think the objective one is the better fit. The "in between" is a bit repetitive, and saying please before every instruction is unnecessary. The more objective and brief voice gives clear instructions, and I continuously appreciate that.
G-worst(1H2L3M): Once again, the "wishy washy" voice feels the least appropriate of the three. This is because it would probably confuse me if I were walking around and trying to reach a destination. I just want clear instructions, not suggestions.
G-difference: One felt very polite and repetitive, another felt objective but robotic, and one felt like a conversational partner giving suggestions. For the purposes of navigation, it just makes the most sense to receive brief and specific instructions. This helps clarify where I'm going to go.
